rationale.R1.3: The text mentions contrastive disambiguation improving coverage but doesn't explicitly link specific claims to evidence in the text.
rationale.R1.4: The text names one prior work (Patel and Rajagopal) but doesn't clearly articulate the delta or map against other related works.
rationale.R1.7: There is a potential citation error, and the text lacks detailed engagement with relevant literature beyond the single named work.
rationale.RX.1: The text presents findings without explicitly stating uncertainty for specific claims or areas where evidence is lacking.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the requested topic of DISAMBIG-RR and its contributions.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions or harmful content are identified in the provided text.
rationale.RX.5: While the text describes the training procedure, it lacks detailed annotation of source material for non-cosmetic changes.
